mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in over 65% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a win rate above 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate strengthening manufacturing output that translates to higher corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 20-day SPX returns during late-cycle expansions with a Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic activity that supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day SPX direction with an information coefficient consistently near zero.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth surpassing 2.5% annually forces monetary tightening expectations that compress equity multiples, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in over 70% of periods where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and monthly retail sales growth validate sustained aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising GDPC1 and RSAFS yields a negative 20-day SPX win rate below 45% in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals weakening manufacturing capacity that reduces near-term corporate revenue forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns during post-shock recovery phases with a win rate below 40%.
